channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Why CBS stands at the epicenter of Trump's assault on the media
item.description: CBS News appears likely to settle a lawsuit brought by President Trump in a case that legal observers say it should win. The network is caught up in Trump's broader assault on the media and an owner intent on finalizing a sale.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: As 'Fork' resignation deadline arrives, a federal judge will consider a stay
item.description: The Trump administration has given more than 2 million federal employees until Thursday to decide whether to stay or go. A last-minute court hearing may throw a wrench into the administration's plans.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump plan offering incentives for federal workers to resign
item.description: A federal judge has blocked President Donald Trump’s plan to push out federal employees by offering them financial incentives.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Collin Binkley And Byron Tau, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Department of Education: The arguments for, against killing it
item.description: Former Trump campaign surrogate Scotti Nell Hughes says the move would bring more accountability at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: US officials now say Trump only wants to displace Palestinians from Gaza temporarily
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — President Donald Trump’s top diplomat and his main spokesperson on Wednesday walked back the idea that he wants the permanent relocation of Palestinians from Gaza, after American allies and even Republican lawmakers rebuffed his suggestion that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protesters rally against Trump's stop-work order for USAID
item.description: USAID and nonprofit workers gathered near the Capitol in D.C. to protest Elon Musk's efforts to shut down the aid agency with his Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: New attorney general moves to align Justice Department with Trump's priorities
item.description: Trump and Attorney General Pam Bondi have both argued the Justice Department under Biden unfairly targeted conservatives, most notably Trump himself.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota House DFL, GOP strike agreement to end stalemate
item.description: A standoff that has stretched on for more than three weeks is ending in the Minnesota House. A deal will get the chamber up and running.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Nevada proposal would add selling deadly fentanyl to first-degree murder charge
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend a charge of first-degree murder to include providing fentanyl to a person who dies from an overdose.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump unleashes a supercharged MAGA agenda and Republicans come aboard
item.description: President Donald Trump is testing how far Republicans are willing to go in supporting his supercharged “Make America Great Again” agenda, tearing down government agencies and taking actions fundamentally at odds with once-traditional party principles.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump OPM buyout offers leave federal workers with risky decisions
item.description: The Trump administration and federal employee unions are in a head-to-head lobbying battle as workers face a Feb. 6 deadline to decide whether they want to accept a government buyout.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says DOGE will make 'rapid safety upgrades' to air traffic control system
item.description: "With the support of President @realDonaldTrump, the @DOGE team will aim to make rapid safety upgrades to the air traffic control system," Musk wrote Wednesday on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Callers swamp Senate offices in Trump's first weeks, leading to busy signals and full voicemail
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Callers are getting busy signals and voicemail inboxes are full at many U.S. Senate offices as people try to reach out and voice their opinions on President Donald Trump’s Cabinet picks, executive orders and moves to dismantle various federal programs.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan's Arab American community offers muted response to Trump's Gaza takeover plan
item.description: DEARBORN, Mich. (AP) — Residents of the largest Arab American community in the U.S. had plenty to say during the 2024 presidential campaign about the roiling politics in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: People gather at Minnesota Capitol in nationwide protest against President Trump
item.description: Protesters marched to the Capitol from St. Paul College, carrying signs denouncing policies on immigration, LGBTQ+ rights and federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina and Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Democratic senators protest after they say Trump gives Musk's staff access to classified info
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats on the Senate Intelligence Committee are demanding answers after they say President Donald Trump gave billionaire Elon Musk and his staff access to sensitive data and classified secrets as part of their work to overhaul the federal government.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Catholic aid group downsizes by nearly 50% amid USAID freeze
item.description: The group started shutting down programs funded by USAID, which supplied about half of its $1.5 billion budget.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE actions 'long overdue, much welcomed': House speaker
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is defending the Elon Musk-led DOGE, saying the government needs to spend money better.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Dept. official accuses FBI chief of 'insubordination,' tamps down talk of revenge on agents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top Justice Department official accused the FBI's acting leaders of “insubordination” in a Wednesday memo in which he sought to soothe anxiety inside the bureau over the potential for a broad purge of agents involved in investigating the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News hires president's daughter-in-law Lara Trump for weekend show on network
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fox News Channel has hired the president's daughter-in-law, Lara Trump, to be host of a new weekend show that will debut later this month.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell slips and falls outside Senate Chamber
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) slipped and fell on the stairs leaving the Senate Chamber on Wednesday afternoon, prompting concerned aides to rush to help the 82-year-old lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Black church in DC that was vandalized by the Proud Boys gains control over the group's trademark
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A judge has awarded a historic Black church in Washington control over the Proud Boys trademark after the far-right group defaulted on a $2.8 million judgment. The Monday ruling in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Neil Jacobs to head NOAA, a scientist with 'Sharpiegate' ties
item.description: President Donald Trump has nominated Neil Jacobs to head NOAA. Some former NOAA employees are concerned about Jacobs' scientific integrity after his prior involvement in “Sharpiegate.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda and Julia Simon, MPR News

item.title: Homeland Security's workforce might not be as big as Trump's immigration goals
item.description: Changes to hiring and retention practices grew the department by 19,000 people in four years. But former officials warn that's still not enough for Trump's ambitious policy goals.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Long before RFK Jr., Brainerd fought bitter legal battle over fluoride in its water supply
item.description: The topic of whether to fluoridate drinking water is back in the public sphere again, in part because of the nomination of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as Health and Human Services Secretary.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Nevada highway projects stuck as 90-day review plays out; Brightline effects uncertain
item.description: Nevada is waiting for a sign from the federal government: Can we count on funding or not? "So, we have pulled in a lot of what we are doing," NDOT Director Tracy Larkin Thomason told lawmakers on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nassau police to help ICE detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes
item.description: NASSAU COUNTY, N.Y. (PIX11) – The Nassau County Police Department will be directly participating in ICE enforcement to detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes, County Executive Bruce Blakeman announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Who is Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy? You may recognize him from MTV
item.description: Duffy, formerly a reality TV star and Wisconsin congressman, was confirmed as the new transportation secretary on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Fox 5

item.title: Mexican border cities can expect higher unemployment, bankruptcies with Trump tariffs, Baja business leaders say
item.description: According to Vega Álvarez, consumers on both sides of the border will be the ones who will pay for the tariffs as "things will have to be overpriced.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5
